context.docs: CLAUDE.md (living source of truth — overrides the spec on conflict) + CDP-BUILD-SPEC.md §9B (campaign workflow engine), §8 (rule-AST→SQL compiler, reused for branch conditions), §9 (outbox→Dispatcher pipeline)
env: Monorepo: pnpm workspaces + Turborepo + Vitest + strict TS. Run from repo root. Node 20+.

Real Postgres (dev): DATABASE_URL=postgres://postgres:postgres@localhost:5433/cdp (migrations applied). Single test file: pnpm --filter <pkg> exec vitest run path. Whole suite: DATABASE_URL=… pnpm test. Typecheck: pnpm typecheck. Lint: pnpm lint.

Browser e2e: pnpm --filter @cdp/web test:e2e — its OWN DB cdp_e2e on its OWN ports (local-api :8788, web :5174), runs with LOCAL_SES_FORCE_MOCK=1 LOCAL_SES_DKIM_STATUS=SUCCESS; re-seeds web/e2e/seed.ts each run. The dev stack (cdp, :8787/:5173) coexists. Playwright config has retries:1.
Dev API for manual checks: pnpm --filter @cdp/service-local-api dev:api (background, restart after backend edits). Health: GET http://localhost:8787/health.

Key reuse points: services/campaign-runner (dsl.ts, core.ts, run.ts, enroll.ts), services/local-api (handlers.ts, routes.ts, app.ts, server.ts), services/dispatcher (core.ts, dispatch.ts), packages/segments (AST + compiler + rule builder), packages/email, web/src/screens (BroadcastComposer email-instance flow, TemplateEditor, SegmentBuilder), web/src/ui/kit.tsx (ActionMenu, Drawer, Button, Field, Select), web/e2e/seed.ts.
invariants: Tenant isolation: every tenant-scoped row carries workspace_id NOT NULL and every query filters by workspace_id. The campaign-runner + enrollment + handlers connect with the Supabase SERVICE ROLE which BYPASSES RLS — they MUST scope by workspace_id IN CODE (RLS is not their safety net). workspace_id is loaded FROM the enrollment/campaign row, never assumed.

workspace_id is NEVER client-supplied — it comes from the authorizer-injected claim (admin API) or a trusted server-set field (the enrollment row). Never trust a workspace_id in a request body. Reject a cross-workspace template_id / segment_id / sender_id (inv.2) — like validateSenderId does today.

All campaign action SENDS go through the SAME outbox → Dispatcher pipeline as broadcasts (§9): suppression → frequency-cap → quiet-hours, gated on a VERIFIED sending domain. A send node carries an email INSTANCE whose envelope (real named From sender + To + Subject) is ALL mandatory — NO no-reply fallback (mirror sendBroadcast gating). The Dispatcher renders merge tags in the SUBJECT, To AND body.

Runner idempotency + concurrency (unchanged guarantee): one tick advances a due enrollment exactly once (SELECT … FOR UPDATE row lock in one tx, re-check active inside the lock; legacy CAS path for unit tests). Outbox dedupe_key per (campaign_id, profile_id, node_id) prevents double-sends on retry. MAX_STEPS_PER_TICK guards pathological graphs. New node execution must preserve all of this.

Webhook action safety: outbound HTTP ONLY to an explicit per-workspace domain ALLOWLIST; enforce a timeout + BOUNDED retries; block SSRF (no internal/link-local/loopback/169.254 metadata targets); secrets (e.g. auth headers) use the @cdp/db secret-crypto envelope pattern, never returned in plaintext over the API; a webhook failure is ISOLATED (recorded; never crashes the tick or double-advances). LOCAL: the HTTP call is mocked/injected — never hit a real external host in tests.

Workspace timezone governs ALL campaign time math (waits, wait-until, hour-of-day window) — DST-correct (reuse the zoned↔UTC approach from the broadcast scheduler: zonedInputToUtcIso/tzOffsetMs). One configurable timezone per workspace; never per-broadcast guesswork.

The DSL ({startNode, nodes}) is the SINGLE graph model; validateCampaignDefinition is the structural gate (exactly ONE trigger, every edge resolves, a reachable exit, and — extended — NO cycles / NO orphan nodes / down-only). The builder is an AUTO-LAYOUT RENDER of that graph: compute (x,y) from the tree, draw rounded orthogonal connectors, branches fan sideways. NO separate graph model, NO stored coordinates, NO loops/back-edges.

REUSE, do not reinvent (code-quality bar): the broadcast email-INSTANCE/clone flow (kind=copy, source_template_id, cloneTemplate, the From/To/Subject envelope + editor return flow) for send nodes; the segment rule builder + AstNode for the if/condition node; the kit ActionMenu / Drawer / Button (auto-locks on promise-returning onClick) / Field / Select; the Dispatcher core; scopedQuery; runInWorkspaceTx; evaluateRealtimeSegmentsForProfile. Match the surrounding code style, comment density and naming.

UI standing rules: NEVER use native confirm/alert/prompt — use ui/dialog.tsx (askConfirm) or purpose-built modals. EVERY server-calling button shows a spinner + locks until the response (kit Button auto-locks when onClick RETURNS the promise — wire it that way). The email editor autosaves (no manual save). PRESERVE every existing data-testid; add stable testids for new UI. Floating toasts (ui/toast.tsx showToast) for transient feedback.

Test-first (§16A/§18): write the failing acceptance tests FIRST, then implement to green. Do NOT mock Postgres in the integration tier — use the REAL local DB. Mock SES/AWS (aws-sdk-client-mock / LocalStack); the webhook HTTP client is injected/mocked. Playwright (real browser) for UI phases with seeded data + stable selectors. turbo test task is cache:false — keep it. Unique workspace UUIDs + file-local namespaces (events.event_id / ses_message_id are GLOBAL keys).

Process hygiene: BUMP the root package.json version per change (patch=tiny, minor=feature, major only when told). UPDATE CLAUDE.md when behavior/architecture changes. Apply new SQL migrations to BOTH the dev DB (cdp) AND the e2e DB (cdp_e2e) via a node pg script (psql is unavailable). RESTART the dev API (pnpm --filter @cdp/service-local-api dev:api — a long-lived tsx process, NOT watch) after backend edits, and REBUILD a changed package (tsc -b) the local-api imports from dist. Scope discipline: build EXACTLY the agreed design — no extra features.
context.loop: This phase has a FRONTEND/UI surface. The widest quality loop is a REAL BROWSER: build the UI in /web (Preact) and verify with Playwright e2e (pnpm --filter @cdp/web test:e2e) against the e2e stack (cdp_e2e DB, :8788/:5174). Reuse the kit (ActionMenu/Drawer/Button/Field/Select), the broadcast email-instance flow, and the segment rule builder. Seed deterministic data (web/e2e/seed.ts) + stable data-testid selectors. NO native dialogs; server buttons spinner+lock.

criteria: The campaigns screen is a LIST (like broadcasts); creating/opening a campaign routes to a SEPARATE /campaigns/:id builder page; there is NO "Design email" button on the campaigns list page (email design is only inside a send-node editor). Verified in a REAL browser.

The campaign list shows lifecycle status + enrollment counts; publish/pause/resume work and are capability-gated + workspace-scoped; an active campaign enrolls + advances profiles. Verified in a REAL browser + against REAL Postgres.

The full journey acceptance test passes: a built campaign enrolls (segment/event/manual), advances through wait/hour-window/if/send/webhook/exit with an injected clock, sends via the Dispatcher (messages_log, campaign_id) and fires the mocked webhook, idempotently, all workspace-scoped.
CLAUDE.md updated; root version bumped; no regression across the full Vitest + e2e suite (cold).
phase.refs: §9B + CLAUDE.md (broadcast list/lifecycle patterns, activity log, dashboards)
scope: Campaigns is a LIST page that MIRRORS Broadcasts (BroadcastComposer): the /campaigns route shows a table/list of campaigns (name, lifecycle status, enrollment counts) with a "New campaign" action; opening/creating a campaign navigates to a SEPARATE edit page at route /campaigns/:id (the canvas builder) — the builder is NOT the top-level campaigns page. REMOVE any top-level "Design email" affordance from the campaigns list/page: email design lives ONLY inside a send-node editor (reached from a send node in the builder), never as a standalone campaigns-page button. Reuse the broadcasts list/detail routing + kit (ActionMenu row actions, etc.).

Campaign LIFECYCLE: draft / active / paused (and the existing completed/exited/failed enrollment states); publish (validate→active), pause/resume, archive. Show per-campaign enrollment counts (active / completed / exited / failed) and optionally per-node counts.

A full END-TO-END journey acceptance test against the local stack: define a campaign in the builder (trigger → wait → hour-window → if → update-profile → send + webhook → exit), enroll a profile (each trigger kind — INCLUDING POSTing to the live local-api /profiles/:id/events path to assert event-trigger enrollment, closing the phase-3 coverage gap), advance via the runner (injected clock), and assert the update-profile step wrote an event-sourced attribute, it sends through the Dispatcher (messages_log) + fires the (mocked) webhook + completes — workspace-scoped, idempotent.
Docs + hygiene: update CLAUDE.md (the campaign builder + new nodes + tz + triggers), bump the root version, ensure migrations applied to cdp + cdp_e2e.
phase.title: Lifecycle, dashboards + full end-to-end journey

e2eFlows: List/detail split: nav-campaigns → list screen (no canvas, no design-email) → New campaign → /campaigns/new builder → save → back to list; open a row → /campaigns/:id builder.
Lifecycle via ActionMenu: publish (draft→active), pause (active→paused), resume (paused→active), archive (→archived, row leaves default list); each spins+locks; archive confirmed via app-dialog.
Capability visibility: marketer sees lifecycle actions; accounting does not (role-visibility pattern).
Full-node journey build smoke: assemble trigger→wait→hour-window→if→update-profile→send→webhook→exit, configure each via its drawer, attach a sendable template, publish to active.
Enrollment counts: a row shows {active/completed/exited/failed} reflecting campaign_enrollments after an enroll.
implUnits: 1. (TEST-FIRST) Write the failing tests above. Start with services/local-api/test/campaign-lifecycle.integration.test.ts + campaign-enrollment-counts.integration.test.ts + the journey integration test; then the e2e specs; then paused-campaign-not-advanced.

2. Backend lifecycle endpoints (services/local-api): add handlers pauseCampaign/resumeCampaign/archiveCampaign (scopedQuery UPDATE campaigns SET status WHERE id; 404 on cross-ws; typed 409 for an illegal transition via nextLifecycle). Register routes POST /campaigns/:id/{pause,resume,archive} = manage_content in routes.ts and HANDLERS map in handlers.ts. No migration — campaigns.status already documents draft|active|paused|archived.

3. GET /campaigns enrollment counts: extend listCampaigns to LEFT JOIN a workspace-scoped GROUP BY (campaign_id,status) aggregate (or a lateral) and shape via campaignCountsShape → return {id,name,status,counts:{active,completed,exited,failed}}.

4. Runner pause gate (services/campaign-runner/src/run.ts): in the locked read, also SELECT the campaign status; only advance when status='active' (paused/archived → no-op result). Preserve FOR UPDATE/legacy-CAS, MAX_STEPS_PER_TICK, dedupe_key — no change to outbox/webhook semantics.

5. Web: SPLIT CampaignBuilder.tsx into CampaignsList.tsx (the LIST: PageHeader with ONLY 'New campaign' — DELETE the design-email Button — a table/list of rows with status Badge + enrollment-counts cell + an ActionMenu of Open/Pause/Resume/Archive mirroring BroadcastComposer) and CampaignDetail.tsx (the existing canvas builder body keyed by id, with a campaigns-back button). Keep the canvas/palette/node-editor/publish code in the detail component verbatim (move, don't rewrite).

6. Web routing (AppShell.tsx + router): #/campaigns → <CampaignsList/>; #/campaigns/new and #/campaigns/:id → <CampaignDetail id?/> (mirror the broadcasts rest === 'new' branch). Preserve the editorReturn flow: returning from a send-node Design email lands on /campaigns/:id detail (takeReturnedTo) — now it opens the detail page directly instead of the combined screen. navigate('/campaigns/'+id) on row open / after create.
7. Web list actions: wire pause/resume/archive to the new endpoints (api.post, button RETURNS the promise → kit auto-lock), askConfirm for archive, showToast on success/error, reload list after.
8. toneFor: add 'paused' (warn) and 'archived' (neutral/danger) keyword handling in web/src/ui/kit.tsx so the badges color correctly (tiny).

9. Seed (web/e2e/seed.ts): ensure CAMP_A (Welcome journey) stays for the list/detail open test; add a seeded enrollment or two (varied status) so the counts cell is non-zero in e2e; ensure an allowlisted webhook host + a sendable template copy exist for the journey smoke. Keep cdp_e2e re-seed contract.

10. Docs + hygiene: update CLAUDE.md (campaigns = list + /campaigns/:id detail; design-email only in send-node editor; lifecycle pause/resume/archive endpoints + capability; enrollment counts on the list; runner honors campaign status); bump root package.json (minor — feature). Apply NO migration (status already supports the values); if a future archived-filter index is wanted, it's optional and would go to cdp + cdp_e2e via a node pg script.
11. Rebuild changed packages (tsc -b) the local-api imports from dist; restart dev API; run pnpm typecheck + full Vitest + e2e cold.
mocks: SES: aws-sdk-client-mock (mockClient(SESv2/SES SendEmailCommand)) in the dispatcher leg of the journey test — assert SendEmail called with the workspace Configuration Set, only AFTER suppression/cap/quiet-hours/verified-domain gate; NEVER a real send.

Webhook HTTP: an INJECTED WebhookHttpClient (from @cdp/runner-webhook) fake that captures {url, method, headers, body} and can be configured to throw/timeout/return non-2xx — never hits a real host; reuse webhook-node-tick.integration.test's pattern.
SQS: a CapturingSqs stub (push MessageBody) for the runner's dispatch enqueue (no LocalStack needed for the unit/integration tiers here).
Clock: deps.now() injected as a fixed Date per tick (NOT a mock library) — the runner's documented injection point for wait/hour-window determinism.
secret-crypto: reuse the real @cdp/db decryptSecret/isEncryptedSecret with a test KEK (CDP_MASTER_KEY) so the webhook auth-header decryption path is exercised, not stubbed.
Real (NOT mocked): Postgres (adminPool / local-api createApp over the real pool) and the real browser (Playwright) for UI tiers.
plan.notes: WHERE 'Design email' is today: CampaignBuilder.tsx PageHeader actions (data-testid="design-email", lines ~266-275) — a standalone Button navigating to /editor. Phase-7 REMOVES this from the LIST page; the SAME design-email affordance must remain reachable INSIDE the send-node editor (NodeEditor's send body already has a Design email path for the campaign send instance — that stays). LIST/DETAIL SPLIT: CampaignBuilder.tsx is currently ONE combined screen — a name/canvas builder on top + a campaign list below — rendered at BOTH #/campaigns and #/campaigns/* (AppShell screenFor returns <CampaignBuilder/> for both). Split it: CampaignsList (list only) at #/campaigns, CampaignDetail (the canvas builder, the bulk of today's file moved verbatim) at #/campaigns/new and #/campaigns/:id, mirroring BroadcastComposer/BroadcastWizard + the AppShell broadcasts branch. The editorReturn flow already targets /campaigns/:id (takeReturnedTo) so returning from a send-node Design email will now naturally land on the detail page. NO MIGRATION NEEDED: campaigns.status (0005_messaging.sql line 26) already documents draft|active|paused|archived and is a free-text NOT NULL DEFAULT 'draft' column; campaign_enrollments.status already covers active|completed|exited|failed — pause/resume/archive are plain UPDATEs, enrollment counts a GROUP BY. (If you want to harden, an optional CHECK constraint could be added to cdp+cdp_e2e via a node pg script, but the project convention favors no migration when the column already supports the values — recommend none.) FULL-JOURNEY DRIVER: the runner is advanced with deps.now() returning a FIXED Date per tick (the documented injection point in RunDeps; full-lifecycle.integration.test already does t0/t2). The journey test composes the existing real-Postgres patterns into ONE end-to-end: enroll via the LIVE local-api POST /profiles/:id/events (closes the phase-3 gap — handlers.ts sendProfileEvent already calls enrollFromEvent on the same tx, so this is exercising the wired path, not new code) PLUS manual+segment enroll, then tick through wait→hour-window→if→set_attribute→send→webhook→exit, asserting: an event-sourced attribute write, an outbox→dispatcher messages_log row with campaign_id (SES mocked, gated), the injected webhook fired once (SSRF/allowlist enforced, failures isolated), idempotent re-tick (dedupe_key + per-node webhook marker), all workspace-scoped (runStatementsInWorkspaceTx guard + a parallel WS_B left untouched). The ONLY backend behavior that may be genuinely missing is the runner reading campaign.status to halt a PAUSED campaign — the paused-campaign-not-advanced test drives adding that gate inside the locked tick (preserving FOR UPDATE/CAS/idempotency); everything else (webhook, set-attribute, send, hour-window, all three enroll kinds) already has green integration coverage, so phase-7's real new surface is the UI list/detail split + lifecycle endpoints/counts + the combined acceptance test.
pureFunctions: campaignCountsShape(rows): fold the GROUP BY (campaign_id,status) rows into a per-campaign {active,completed,exited,failed} record defaulting missing buckets to 0 (testable without DB; used to shape GET /campaigns).

nextLifecycle(current, action): map (status, 'pause'|'resume'|'archive') → next status or a typed rejection reason (e.g. resume only from 'paused'); pure transition table reused by the handler and a unit test.
isEnrollableCampaignStatus(status): boolean — only 'active' enrolls/advances (reused by the pause gate + enroll cores assertion).

REUSE existing pures: computeWaitNextRunAt, parseIso8601DurationSeconds, isWaitElapsed, processNode, evaluateBranch, buildBranchMatchQuery, parseEventEnrollmentTrigger, evaluateEventPayloadFilter, buildEnrollmentInsert, decideReenrollment, zonedInputToUtcIso/tzOffsetMs (hour-window + wait tz math) — no new tz code.
reuse: BroadcastComposer.tsx: the list+detail routing model, ActionMenu row actions, agoLabel/whenLabel, the no-domain banner pattern, status Badge+toneFor, spinner+lock send button wiring — MIRROR for CampaignsList.
AppShell.tsx broadcasts branch (rest === 'new' ? <Wizard/> : <Wizard id/>) — copy for campaigns list vs detail.

campaign-runner: runEnrollment + RunDeps (injected now/sqs/webhookClient/withTx), buildSweepQuery, processNode, computeWaitNextRunAt, hour-window logic, set_attribute event-sourcing, the per-(campaign,profile,node) dedupe_key — the journey test composes these EXACTLY as full-lifecycle.integration.test + webhook-node-tick + set-attribute-event-sourced + hour-window.integration already do.
campaign-send-dispatcher-instance.integration.test.ts: the runner-outbox → dispatcher-core → messages_log(campaign_id) chaining (SES mocked) — the journey test's send leg copies this.

enroll-from-event.integration.test.ts: the enrollFromEvent assertions; the journey test EXTENDS it by driving the LIVE local-api POST /profiles/:id/events (createApp over the real pool) instead of calling the core directly.

local-api handlers: scopedQuery, ownsResource, validateDefinitionOr400, enrollDepsOnClient/enrollDepsOnPool, the existing activateCampaign gate (publish) — the new pause/resume/archive sit beside activate and reuse scopedQuery + the 404 pattern.
ui kit: ActionMenu, Badge, Button (promise auto-lock), PageHeader, EmptyState, Card; ui/dialog askConfirm; ui/toast showToast — all already used by BroadcastComposer.
web/e2e/helpers.ts (loginAs, DEV_MKT/DEV_ACCT) + seed.ts (CAMP_A, DEV_* logins, sendable template) — reuse for the new e2e specs.
@cdp/shared timeZoneList/zonedInputToUtcIso/tzOffsetMs — the ONE tz implementation for hour-window + wait math (no new tz code).

assertions: loginAs(DEV_MKT) → nav-campaigns → the LIST screen renders (new testid campaigns-list-screen / list testid campaign-list), NOT the canvas: page.getByTestId('campaign-canvas') has count 0 and page.getByTestId('campaign-builder') is NOT present on the list route (#/campaigns).
The list page has NO top-level Design email button: await expect(page.getByTestId('design-email')).toHaveCount(0) while on #/campaigns (regression for the removed PageHeader affordance).

The list has a 'New campaign' action (campaign-new); clicking it navigates to a SEPARATE detail route (assert page.url() matches /#\/campaigns\/(new|[0-9a-f-]{36})$/ and campaign-canvas becomes visible) — the builder is NOT the top-level page.

Opening the seeded 'Welcome journey' row (campaign-open / row click) navigates to #/campaigns/<CAMP_A> and the canvas reconstructs the DSL (node-trigger/node-wait/node-condition visible) — list→detail routing mirrors broadcasts.

From inside the detail builder, a send node's editor still exposes Design email (open node-send editor drawer → design-email present) — proving email design lives ONLY inside a send-node editor, not on the list.
Each list row shows the lifecycle status badge (campaign-status) and an enrollment-counts summary cell (new testid campaign-counts showing active/completed/exited/failed).
Back navigation from the detail builder returns to the list (a campaigns-back button mirroring broadcasts-back) → #/campaigns.
testFiles.criterion: The campaigns screen is a LIST (like broadcasts); creating/opening a campaign routes to a SEPARATE /campaigns/:id builder page; there is NO "Design email" button on the campaigns list page (email design is only inside a send-node editor). Verified in a REAL browser.

assertions: Build a minimal valid trigger→send→exit campaign in the detail page, attach a sendable seeded template copy to the send node (reuse the publish-gate spec's attach flow), Publish → status badge flips to 'active' on the list (campaign-status text 'active').

An ActionMenu (campaign-actions kebab, mirroring broadcast-actions) on each list row exposes: Open/Edit, Pause (when active), Resume (when paused), Archive — each a server-calling button that spins+locks (no native confirm; Archive uses askConfirm → app-dialog).

Pause an active campaign → POST /campaigns/:id/pause → status badge becomes 'paused'; Resume → POST /campaigns/:id/resume → back to 'active'; Archive → POST /campaigns/:id/archive → status 'archived' and the row drops from the default list (or shows archived filter).

Capability gating: log in as DEV_ACCT (accounting, lacks manage_content) — the Pause/Resume/Publish/Archive actions are absent/disabled and a direct POST returns 403 (assert via role-visibility pattern); the marketer (manage_content) sees and can use them.
Workspace-scoping: the WS_A campaign list never shows WS_B campaigns; switching workspace re-scopes the list (AppShell key remounts).

Enrollment counts render live: after enrolling a profile (via manual enroll in the builder or seeded enrollment) the row's campaign-counts active count reflects campaign_enrollments grouped by status for that campaign only (workspace-scoped).
testFiles.criterion: The campaign list shows lifecycle status + enrollment counts; publish/pause/resume work and are capability-gated + workspace-scoped; an active campaign enrolls + advances profiles. Verified in a REAL browser.

assertions: POST /campaigns/:id/pause on an 'active' campaign sets status='paused' (UPDATE ... SET status='paused' WHERE id AND workspace_id); returns the new status; idempotent (pausing a paused campaign is a no-op 200, not an error).
POST /campaigns/:id/resume on a 'paused' campaign sets status='active'; resume on a non-paused (draft/archived) campaign is rejected 409 with a typed reason (can only resume a paused campaign).

POST /campaigns/:id/archive sets status='archived' from any state; an archived campaign is excluded from enrollment (enrollFromEvent/enrollFromSegmentChange only target status='active') — regression-assert no new enrollment after archive.

Pause does NOT touch in-flight campaign_enrollments rows directly; assert the runner skips a paused campaign's enrollments (the sweep/runEnrollment re-checks campaign status='active' inside the lock; a paused campaign's due enrollment is left parked, not advanced) — drives this with the campaign-runner core in the journey test, asserted here at the handler level that status flips only.

Tenant isolation (inv.1/inv.2): a pause/resume/archive POST with a :id belonging to WS_B under a WS_A token returns 404 (scopedQuery WHERE workspace_id = ctx.workspaceId AND id = $1, rowCount 0 → 404); workspace_id is never taken from the body.
Capability: routes.ts maps POST /campaigns/:id/{pause,resume,archive} to manage_content; a marketer passes, an accounting role 403s (dispatch enforce).
testFiles.criterion: publish/pause/resume/archive work and are capability-gated + workspace-scoped (REAL Postgres).

assertions: GET /campaigns returns each campaign with status AND a counts object {active, completed, exited, failed} aggregated from campaign_enrollments grouped by status, scoped to ctx.workspaceId and campaign_id (LEFT JOIN / lateral count so a campaign with no enrollments returns all-zero counts).

Counts are per-campaign and workspace-scoped: seed two campaigns in WS_A with differing enrollment status mixes + one campaign in WS_B; assert WS_A list shows the correct per-campaign split and never sums WS_B rows.

A single workspace-scoped aggregate query (one round-trip, GROUP BY campaign_id, status) — assert correctness for active/completed/exited/failed buckets and that an unknown status (defensive) does not break the shape.
GET /campaigns/:id continues to return the full definition + timezone unchanged (regression).
testFiles.criterion: The campaign list shows lifecycle status + enrollment counts (REAL Postgres).

assertions: Setup against REAL Postgres (adminPool): one workspace (settings.timezone set, e.g. 'America/New_York'), a profile with email, a verified sending_domain + domain_senders row, an email_templates copy (kind='copy') with subject+sender_id+to_address set (sendable), and a campaign whose definition is trigger(event)→wait→hour_of_day_window→condition→action(set_attribute)→action(send)→action(webhook)→exit, status='active', with the webhook url on a per-workspace allowlist host.

LIVE EVENT ENROLLMENT (closes the phase-3 gap): drive enrollment through the local-api createApp() by POSTing to POST /profiles/:id/events (the live route, marketer token) with the trigger eventType+payload → assert exactly ONE campaign_enrollments row at current_node=trigger, status='active', workspace-scoped; replaying the same POST (different event_id) does NOT duplicate (ON CONFLICT once).

ALSO assert the other two trigger kinds enroll via their cores in the same workspace: enrollProfileManually (POST /campaigns/:id/enroll body {profile_id}) and enrollFromSegmentChange — each idempotent, each one row.

ADVANCE with an injected clock: build RunDeps with now()=fixed Date (reuse withWorkspaceTx/runStatementsInWorkspaceTx + an injected webhookClient capturing requests + a CapturingSqs). Tick at T0 → parks at wait; tick at T0+delay but OUTSIDE the hour window → parks/defers at hour_of_day_window (next_run_at moved into the window, DST-correct in the workspace tz); tick at a clock INSIDE the window → condition evaluates, set_attribute writes, send enqueues to outbox, webhook fires, enrollment completes.

UPDATE-PROFILE is event-sourced: assert set_attribute wrote BOTH the profiles.attributes change AND an events row (event-sourced attribute, per set-attribute-event-sourced.integration.test pattern) so it flows into the behavioral timeline; the attribute is readable on the profile.

SEND through the Dispatcher: take the outbox row the runner produced (campaign_id set, dedupe_key = campaign:<camp>:<profile>:<sendNodeId>) and run the dispatcher core (SES mocked via aws-sdk-client-mock, suppression→cap→quiet-hours→verified-domain gate) → assert a messages_log row with campaign_id = the campaign, ses_message_id set, status='sent', and SES SendEmail called with the workspace Configuration Set AFTER the gates (never a real send).

WEBHOOK fired via the INJECTED client (never a real host): assert exactly one captured request to the allowlisted URL, method/headers/rendered body correct ({{customer.*}} merged), the encrypted auth header decrypted at call time only; an SSRF/blocked target (loopback/169.254/non-allowlist) is refused and the failure is ISOLATED (recorded in activity_log source='webhook', tick does not crash, enrollment still advances).

IDEMPOTENCY: re-running the final tick (same enrollmentId) does NOT create a second outbox row (UNIQUE dedupe_key), does NOT re-send (messages_log stays 1), does NOT re-fire the webhook (per-node dedupe marker), and the enrollment stays 'completed'.

WORKSPACE-SCOPING: a parallel WS_B campaign/enrollment with identical structure is untouched by the WS_A ticks; every write asserted scoped to the enrollment's workspace_id (runStatementsInWorkspaceTx guard); the dispatcher messages_log is in WS_A only.
testFiles.criterion: The full journey acceptance test passes: a built campaign enrolls (segment/event/manual), advances through wait/hour-window/if/send/webhook/exit with an injected clock, sends via the Dispatcher (messages_log, campaign_id) and fires the mocked webhook, idempotently, all workspace-scoped.

assertions: On #/campaigns/new build trigger→wait→hour_of_day_window→if→update-profile→send→webhook→exit via the (+) palette (reuse campaign-builder spec helpers), configure each node via its drawer editor (reuse campaign-node-editors patterns), attach a sendable template to the send node, configure the webhook node URL to a seeded-allowlisted host.
Save round-trips (toast); Publish flips status to 'active' on the list; the list row shows status 'active' + zero-initialized enrollment counts.
This is a thin browser smoke ONLY (no runner tick in-browser) — the advance/send/webhook assertions live in the integration test above.
testFiles.criterion: Full journey UI smoke: build the full-node journey in the browser and publish it (the runner advance is covered by the integration test).

assertions: Seed an active campaign + an enrollment due now; pause the campaign (status='paused'); runEnrollment(injected now) for that enrollment → result is a no-op/'skipped' (enrollment left at its node, status unchanged, no outbox/send) because the in-tx re-check requires the campaign status='active'.
Resume (status='active') then runEnrollment again → it advances normally (proves pause is a reversible halt, not a data mutation on the enrollment).

If the current runner does NOT yet read campaign.status inside the tick, this is the failing test that drives adding that gate in run.ts (load campaign status alongside definition in the locked read; honor 'active' only); idempotency/concurrency guarantees preserved.
testFiles.criterion: Lifecycle pause halts advancement: a paused campaign's due enrollment is not advanced by the runner.

instructions: Read CLAUDE.md (living source of truth — overrides the spec on conflict) + CDP-BUILD-SPEC.md §9B (campaign workflow engine), §8 (rule-AST→SQL compiler, reused for branch conditions), §9 (outbox→Dispatcher pipeline) + the cited files: §9B + CLAUDE.md (broadcast list/lifecycle patterns, activity log, dashboards). Reuse the existing campaign-runner / dispatcher / segment compiler / broadcast email-instance flow / UI kit per the plan's "reuse" list.
FIRST write the failing tests from the plan (red), then implement to green. Logic in pure injected functions; handlers/components thin.

Honor every invariant — tenant isolation, workspace_id never client-supplied, all sends through the Dispatcher gating, runner idempotency/concurrency, webhook SSRF/allowlist + injected HTTP, workspace-tz time math, the single DSL graph model (no stored coords/loops/orphans), reuse + UI standing rules.

Mock AWS/SES (aws-sdk-client-mock) or LocalStack; the integration tier uses the REAL local Postgres (never mock the DB). The webhook HTTP client is injected/mocked. For UI phases verify with Playwright (real browser) against the e2e stack; seed deterministic data.

Apply any new SQL migration to BOTH cdp AND cdp_e2e (node pg script; psql unavailable). Run the relevant pnpm test (with DATABASE_URL) + pnpm typecheck + pnpm lint, and Playwright e2e for UI phases. Rebuild a changed package (tsc -b) and RESTART the dev API if you manually exercise it. Iterate until GREEN incl. a COLD run; confirm no regression. Report actual command output.
Bump the root package.json version appropriately and update CLAUDE.md if behavior/architecture changed. Implement ONLY the agreed design — no scope creep. Return ONLY the JSON.
prompt.outputFormat: JSON: { filesCreated: string[], filesModified: string[], migrationsApplied: string[], versionBump: string, testsPass: boolean, testSummary: string, e2eSummary: string, typecheckOk: boolean, lintOk: boolean, commandsRun: string[], notes: string }
prompt.role: senior full-stack TypeScript engineer building a serverless multi-tenant CDP campaign engine + Preact SPA, test-first
prompt.task: Implement "Lifecycle, dashboards + full end-to-end journey" to satisfy its test plan and acceptance criteria. Red → green → refactor. REUSE existing infra; do not reinvent.

title: Implement: Lifecycle, dashboards + full end-to-end journey (iteration 1)

criteria: The campaigns screen is a LIST (like broadcasts); creating/opening a campaign routes to a SEPARATE /campaigns/:id builder page; there is NO "Design email" button on the campaigns list page (email design is only inside a send-node editor). Verified in a REAL browser.

The campaign list shows lifecycle status + enrollment counts; publish/pause/resume work and are capability-gated + workspace-scoped; an active campaign enrolls + advances profiles. Verified in a REAL browser + against REAL Postgres.

The full journey acceptance test passes: a built campaign enrolls (segment/event/manual), advances through wait/hour-window/if/send/webhook/exit with an injected clock, sends via the Dispatcher (messages_log, campaign_id) and fires the mocked webhook, idempotently, all workspace-scoped.
CLAUDE.md updated; root version bumped; no regression across the full Vitest + e2e suite (cold).
phase.refs: §9B + CLAUDE.md (broadcast list/lifecycle patterns, activity log, dashboards)
scope: Campaigns is a LIST page that MIRRORS Broadcasts (BroadcastComposer): the /campaigns route shows a table/list of campaigns (name, lifecycle status, enrollment counts) with a "New campaign" action; opening/creating a campaign navigates to a SEPARATE edit page at route /campaigns/:id (the canvas builder) — the builder is NOT the top-level campaigns page. REMOVE any top-level "Design email" affordance from the campaigns list/page: email design lives ONLY inside a send-node editor (reached from a send node in the builder), never as a standalone campaigns-page button. Reuse the broadcasts list/detail routing + kit (ActionMenu row actions, etc.).

Campaign LIFECYCLE: draft / active / paused (and the existing completed/exited/failed enrollment states); publish (validate→active), pause/resume, archive. Show per-campaign enrollment counts (active / completed / exited / failed) and optionally per-node counts.

A full END-TO-END journey acceptance test against the local stack: define a campaign in the builder (trigger → wait → hour-window → if → update-profile → send + webhook → exit), enroll a profile (each trigger kind — INCLUDING POSTing to the live local-api /profiles/:id/events path to assert event-trigger enrollment, closing the phase-3 coverage gap), advance via the runner (injected clock), and assert the update-profile step wrote an event-sourced attribute, it sends through the Dispatcher (messages_log) + fires the (mocked) webhook + completes — workspace-scoped, idempotent.
Docs + hygiene: update CLAUDE.md (the campaign builder + new nodes + tz + triggers), bump the root version, ensure migrations applied to cdp + cdp_e2e.
phase.title: Lifecycle, dashboards + full end-to-end journey

e2eFlows: List/detail split: nav-campaigns → list screen (no canvas, no design-email) → New campaign → /campaigns/new builder → save → back to list; open a row → /campaigns/:id builder.
Lifecycle via ActionMenu: publish (draft→active), pause (active→paused), resume (paused→active), archive (→archived, row leaves default list); each spins+locks; archive confirmed via app-dialog.
Capability visibility: marketer sees lifecycle actions; accounting does not (role-visibility pattern).
Full-node journey build smoke: assemble trigger→wait→hour-window→if→update-profile→send→webhook→exit, configure each via its drawer, attach a sendable template, publish to active.
Enrollment counts: a row shows {active/completed/exited/failed} reflecting campaign_enrollments after an enroll.
implUnits: 1. (TEST-FIRST) Write the failing tests above. Start with services/local-api/test/campaign-lifecycle.integration.test.ts + campaign-enrollment-counts.integration.test.ts + the journey integration test; then the e2e specs; then paused-campaign-not-advanced.

2. Backend lifecycle endpoints (services/local-api): add handlers pauseCampaign/resumeCampaign/archiveCampaign (scopedQuery UPDATE campaigns SET status WHERE id; 404 on cross-ws; typed 409 for an illegal transition via nextLifecycle). Register routes POST /campaigns/:id/{pause,resume,archive} = manage_content in routes.ts and HANDLERS map in handlers.ts. No migration — campaigns.status already documents draft|active|paused|archived.

3. GET /campaigns enrollment counts: extend listCampaigns to LEFT JOIN a workspace-scoped GROUP BY (campaign_id,status) aggregate (or a lateral) and shape via campaignCountsShape → return {id,name,status,counts:{active,completed,exited,failed}}.

4. Runner pause gate (services/campaign-runner/src/run.ts): in the locked read, also SELECT the campaign status; only advance when status='active' (paused/archived → no-op result). Preserve FOR UPDATE/legacy-CAS, MAX_STEPS_PER_TICK, dedupe_key — no change to outbox/webhook semantics.

5. Web: SPLIT CampaignBuilder.tsx into CampaignsList.tsx (the LIST: PageHeader with ONLY 'New campaign' — DELETE the design-email Button — a table/list of rows with status Badge + enrollment-counts cell + an ActionMenu of Open/Pause/Resume/Archive mirroring BroadcastComposer) and CampaignDetail.tsx (the existing canvas builder body keyed by id, with a campaigns-back button). Keep the canvas/palette/node-editor/publish code in the detail component verbatim (move, don't rewrite).

6. Web routing (AppShell.tsx + router): #/campaigns → <CampaignsList/>; #/campaigns/new and #/campaigns/:id → <CampaignDetail id?/> (mirror the broadcasts rest === 'new' branch). Preserve the editorReturn flow: returning from a send-node Design email lands on /campaigns/:id detail (takeReturnedTo) — now it opens the detail page directly instead of the combined screen. navigate('/campaigns/'+id) on row open / after create.
7. Web list actions: wire pause/resume/archive to the new endpoints (api.post, button RETURNS the promise → kit auto-lock), askConfirm for archive, showToast on success/error, reload list after.
8. toneFor: add 'paused' (warn) and 'archived' (neutral/danger) keyword handling in web/src/ui/kit.tsx so the badges color correctly (tiny).

9. Seed (web/e2e/seed.ts): ensure CAMP_A (Welcome journey) stays for the list/detail open test; add a seeded enrollment or two (varied status) so the counts cell is non-zero in e2e; ensure an allowlisted webhook host + a sendable template copy exist for the journey smoke. Keep cdp_e2e re-seed contract.

10. Docs + hygiene: update CLAUDE.md (campaigns = list + /campaigns/:id detail; design-email only in send-node editor; lifecycle pause/resume/archive endpoints + capability; enrollment counts on the list; runner honors campaign status); bump root package.json (minor — feature). Apply NO migration (status already supports the values); if a future archived-filter index is wanted, it's optional and would go to cdp + cdp_e2e via a node pg script.
11. Rebuild changed packages (tsc -b) the local-api imports from dist; restart dev API; run pnpm typecheck + full Vitest + e2e cold.
mocks: SES: aws-sdk-client-mock (mockClient(SESv2/SES SendEmailCommand)) in the dispatcher leg of the journey test — assert SendEmail called with the workspace Configuration Set, only AFTER suppression/cap/quiet-hours/verified-domain gate; NEVER a real send.

Webhook HTTP: an INJECTED WebhookHttpClient (from @cdp/runner-webhook) fake that captures {url, method, headers, body} and can be configured to throw/timeout/return non-2xx — never hits a real host; reuse webhook-node-tick.integration.test's pattern.
SQS: a CapturingSqs stub (push MessageBody) for the runner's dispatch enqueue (no LocalStack needed for the unit/integration tiers here).
Clock: deps.now() injected as a fixed Date per tick (NOT a mock library) — the runner's documented injection point for wait/hour-window determinism.
secret-crypto: reuse the real @cdp/db decryptSecret/isEncryptedSecret with a test KEK (CDP_MASTER_KEY) so the webhook auth-header decryption path is exercised, not stubbed.
Real (NOT mocked): Postgres (adminPool / local-api createApp over the real pool) and the real browser (Playwright) for UI tiers.
plan.notes: WHERE 'Design email' is today: CampaignBuilder.tsx PageHeader actions (data-testid="design-email", lines ~266-275) — a standalone Button navigating to /editor. Phase-7 REMOVES this from the LIST page; the SAME design-email affordance must remain reachable INSIDE the send-node editor (NodeEditor's send body already has a Design email path for the campaign send instance — that stays). LIST/DETAIL SPLIT: CampaignBuilder.tsx is currently ONE combined screen — a name/canvas builder on top + a campaign list below — rendered at BOTH #/campaigns and #/campaigns/* (AppShell screenFor returns <CampaignBuilder/> for both). Split it: CampaignsList (list only) at #/campaigns, CampaignDetail (the canvas builder, the bulk of today's file moved verbatim) at #/campaigns/new and #/campaigns/:id, mirroring BroadcastComposer/BroadcastWizard + the AppShell broadcasts branch. The editorReturn flow already targets /campaigns/:id (takeReturnedTo) so returning from a send-node Design email will now naturally land on the detail page. NO MIGRATION NEEDED: campaigns.status (0005_messaging.sql line 26) already documents draft|active|paused|archived and is a free-text NOT NULL DEFAULT 'draft' column; campaign_enrollments.status already covers active|completed|exited|failed — pause/resume/archive are plain UPDATEs, enrollment counts a GROUP BY. (If you want to harden, an optional CHECK constraint could be added to cdp+cdp_e2e via a node pg script, but the project convention favors no migration when the column already supports the values — recommend none.) FULL-JOURNEY DRIVER: the runner is advanced with deps.now() returning a FIXED Date per tick (the documented injection point in RunDeps; full-lifecycle.integration.test already does t0/t2). The journey test composes the existing real-Postgres patterns into ONE end-to-end: enroll via the LIVE local-api POST /profiles/:id/events (closes the phase-3 gap — handlers.ts sendProfileEvent already calls enrollFromEvent on the same tx, so this is exercising the wired path, not new code) PLUS manual+segment enroll, then tick through wait→hour-window→if→set_attribute→send→webhook→exit, asserting: an event-sourced attribute write, an outbox→dispatcher messages_log row with campaign_id (SES mocked, gated), the injected webhook fired once (SSRF/allowlist enforced, failures isolated), idempotent re-tick (dedupe_key + per-node webhook marker), all workspace-scoped (runStatementsInWorkspaceTx guard + a parallel WS_B left untouched). The ONLY backend behavior that may be genuinely missing is the runner reading campaign.status to halt a PAUSED campaign — the paused-campaign-not-advanced test drives adding that gate inside the locked tick (preserving FOR UPDATE/CAS/idempotency); everything else (webhook, set-attribute, send, hour-window, all three enroll kinds) already has green integration coverage, so phase-7's real new surface is the UI list/detail split + lifecycle endpoints/counts + the combined acceptance test.
pureFunctions: campaignCountsShape(rows): fold the GROUP BY (campaign_id,status) rows into a per-campaign {active,completed,exited,failed} record defaulting missing buckets to 0 (testable without DB; used to shape GET /campaigns).

nextLifecycle(current, action): map (status, 'pause'|'resume'|'archive') → next status or a typed rejection reason (e.g. resume only from 'paused'); pure transition table reused by the handler and a unit test.
isEnrollableCampaignStatus(status): boolean — only 'active' enrolls/advances (reused by the pause gate + enroll cores assertion).

REUSE existing pures: computeWaitNextRunAt, parseIso8601DurationSeconds, isWaitElapsed, processNode, evaluateBranch, buildBranchMatchQuery, parseEventEnrollmentTrigger, evaluateEventPayloadFilter, buildEnrollmentInsert, decideReenrollment, zonedInputToUtcIso/tzOffsetMs (hour-window + wait tz math) — no new tz code.
reuse: BroadcastComposer.tsx: the list+detail routing model, ActionMenu row actions, agoLabel/whenLabel, the no-domain banner pattern, status Badge+toneFor, spinner+lock send button wiring — MIRROR for CampaignsList.
AppShell.tsx broadcasts branch (rest === 'new' ? <Wizard/> : <Wizard id/>) — copy for campaigns list vs detail.

campaign-runner: runEnrollment + RunDeps (injected now/sqs/webhookClient/withTx), buildSweepQuery, processNode, computeWaitNextRunAt, hour-window logic, set_attribute event-sourcing, the per-(campaign,profile,node) dedupe_key — the journey test composes these EXACTLY as full-lifecycle.integration.test + webhook-node-tick + set-attribute-event-sourced + hour-window.integration already do.
campaign-send-dispatcher-instance.integration.test.ts: the runner-outbox → dispatcher-core → messages_log(campaign_id) chaining (SES mocked) — the journey test's send leg copies this.

enroll-from-event.integration.test.ts: the enrollFromEvent assertions; the journey test EXTENDS it by driving the LIVE local-api POST /profiles/:id/events (createApp over the real pool) instead of calling the core directly.

local-api handlers: scopedQuery, ownsResource, validateDefinitionOr400, enrollDepsOnClient/enrollDepsOnPool, the existing activateCampaign gate (publish) — the new pause/resume/archive sit beside activate and reuse scopedQuery + the 404 pattern.
ui kit: ActionMenu, Badge, Button (promise auto-lock), PageHeader, EmptyState, Card; ui/dialog askConfirm; ui/toast showToast — all already used by BroadcastComposer.
web/e2e/helpers.ts (loginAs, DEV_MKT/DEV_ACCT) + seed.ts (CAMP_A, DEV_* logins, sendable template) — reuse for the new e2e specs.
@cdp/shared timeZoneList/zonedInputToUtcIso/tzOffsetMs — the ONE tz implementation for hour-window + wait math (no new tz code).

assertions: loginAs(DEV_MKT) → nav-campaigns → the LIST screen renders (new testid campaigns-list-screen / list testid campaign-list), NOT the canvas: page.getByTestId('campaign-canvas') has count 0 and page.getByTestId('campaign-builder') is NOT present on the list route (#/campaigns).
The list page has NO top-level Design email button: await expect(page.getByTestId('design-email')).toHaveCount(0) while on #/campaigns (regression for the removed PageHeader affordance).

The list has a 'New campaign' action (campaign-new); clicking it navigates to a SEPARATE detail route (assert page.url() matches /#\/campaigns\/(new|[0-9a-f-]{36})$/ and campaign-canvas becomes visible) — the builder is NOT the top-level page.

Opening the seeded 'Welcome journey' row (campaign-open / row click) navigates to #/campaigns/<CAMP_A> and the canvas reconstructs the DSL (node-trigger/node-wait/node-condition visible) — list→detail routing mirrors broadcasts.

From inside the detail builder, a send node's editor still exposes Design email (open node-send editor drawer → design-email present) — proving email design lives ONLY inside a send-node editor, not on the list.
Each list row shows the lifecycle status badge (campaign-status) and an enrollment-counts summary cell (new testid campaign-counts showing active/completed/exited/failed).
Back navigation from the detail builder returns to the list (a campaigns-back button mirroring broadcasts-back) → #/campaigns.
testFiles.criterion: The campaigns screen is a LIST (like broadcasts); creating/opening a campaign routes to a SEPARATE /campaigns/:id builder page; there is NO "Design email" button on the campaigns list page (email design is only inside a send-node editor). Verified in a REAL browser.

assertions: Build a minimal valid trigger→send→exit campaign in the detail page, attach a sendable seeded template copy to the send node (reuse the publish-gate spec's attach flow), Publish → status badge flips to 'active' on the list (campaign-status text 'active').

An ActionMenu (campaign-actions kebab, mirroring broadcast-actions) on each list row exposes: Open/Edit, Pause (when active), Resume (when paused), Archive — each a server-calling button that spins+locks (no native confirm; Archive uses askConfirm → app-dialog).

Pause an active campaign → POST /campaigns/:id/pause → status badge becomes 'paused'; Resume → POST /campaigns/:id/resume → back to 'active'; Archive → POST /campaigns/:id/archive → status 'archived' and the row drops from the default list (or shows archived filter).

Capability gating: log in as DEV_ACCT (accounting, lacks manage_content) — the Pause/Resume/Publish/Archive actions are absent/disabled and a direct POST returns 403 (assert via role-visibility pattern); the marketer (manage_content) sees and can use them.
Workspace-scoping: the WS_A campaign list never shows WS_B campaigns; switching workspace re-scopes the list (AppShell key remounts).

Enrollment counts render live: after enrolling a profile (via manual enroll in the builder or seeded enrollment) the row's campaign-counts active count reflects campaign_enrollments grouped by status for that campaign only (workspace-scoped).
testFiles.criterion: The campaign list shows lifecycle status + enrollment counts; publish/pause/resume work and are capability-gated + workspace-scoped; an active campaign enrolls + advances profiles. Verified in a REAL browser.

assertions: POST /campaigns/:id/pause on an 'active' campaign sets status='paused' (UPDATE ... SET status='paused' WHERE id AND workspace_id); returns the new status; idempotent (pausing a paused campaign is a no-op 200, not an error).
POST /campaigns/:id/resume on a 'paused' campaign sets status='active'; resume on a non-paused (draft/archived) campaign is rejected 409 with a typed reason (can only resume a paused campaign).

POST /campaigns/:id/archive sets status='archived' from any state; an archived campaign is excluded from enrollment (enrollFromEvent/enrollFromSegmentChange only target status='active') — regression-assert no new enrollment after archive.

Pause does NOT touch in-flight campaign_enrollments rows directly; assert the runner skips a paused campaign's enrollments (the sweep/runEnrollment re-checks campaign status='active' inside the lock; a paused campaign's due enrollment is left parked, not advanced) — drives this with the campaign-runner core in the journey test, asserted here at the handler level that status flips only.

Tenant isolation (inv.1/inv.2): a pause/resume/archive POST with a :id belonging to WS_B under a WS_A token returns 404 (scopedQuery WHERE workspace_id = ctx.workspaceId AND id = $1, rowCount 0 → 404); workspace_id is never taken from the body.
Capability: routes.ts maps POST /campaigns/:id/{pause,resume,archive} to manage_content; a marketer passes, an accounting role 403s (dispatch enforce).
testFiles.criterion: publish/pause/resume/archive work and are capability-gated + workspace-scoped (REAL Postgres).

assertions: GET /campaigns returns each campaign with status AND a counts object {active, completed, exited, failed} aggregated from campaign_enrollments grouped by status, scoped to ctx.workspaceId and campaign_id (LEFT JOIN / lateral count so a campaign with no enrollments returns all-zero counts).

Counts are per-campaign and workspace-scoped: seed two campaigns in WS_A with differing enrollment status mixes + one campaign in WS_B; assert WS_A list shows the correct per-campaign split and never sums WS_B rows.

A single workspace-scoped aggregate query (one round-trip, GROUP BY campaign_id, status) — assert correctness for active/completed/exited/failed buckets and that an unknown status (defensive) does not break the shape.
GET /campaigns/:id continues to return the full definition + timezone unchanged (regression).
testFiles.criterion: The campaign list shows lifecycle status + enrollment counts (REAL Postgres).

assertions: Setup against REAL Postgres (adminPool): one workspace (settings.timezone set, e.g. 'America/New_York'), a profile with email, a verified sending_domain + domain_senders row, an email_templates copy (kind='copy') with subject+sender_id+to_address set (sendable), and a campaign whose definition is trigger(event)→wait→hour_of_day_window→condition→action(set_attribute)→action(send)→action(webhook)→exit, status='active', with the webhook url on a per-workspace allowlist host.

LIVE EVENT ENROLLMENT (closes the phase-3 gap): drive enrollment through the local-api createApp() by POSTing to POST /profiles/:id/events (the live route, marketer token) with the trigger eventType+payload → assert exactly ONE campaign_enrollments row at current_node=trigger, status='active', workspace-scoped; replaying the same POST (different event_id) does NOT duplicate (ON CONFLICT once).

ALSO assert the other two trigger kinds enroll via their cores in the same workspace: enrollProfileManually (POST /campaigns/:id/enroll body {profile_id}) and enrollFromSegmentChange — each idempotent, each one row.

ADVANCE with an injected clock: build RunDeps with now()=fixed Date (reuse withWorkspaceTx/runStatementsInWorkspaceTx + an injected webhookClient capturing requests + a CapturingSqs). Tick at T0 → parks at wait; tick at T0+delay but OUTSIDE the hour window → parks/defers at hour_of_day_window (next_run_at moved into the window, DST-correct in the workspace tz); tick at a clock INSIDE the window → condition evaluates, set_attribute writes, send enqueues to outbox, webhook fires, enrollment completes.

UPDATE-PROFILE is event-sourced: assert set_attribute wrote BOTH the profiles.attributes change AND an events row (event-sourced attribute, per set-attribute-event-sourced.integration.test pattern) so it flows into the behavioral timeline; the attribute is readable on the profile.

SEND through the Dispatcher: take the outbox row the runner produced (campaign_id set, dedupe_key = campaign:<camp>:<profile>:<sendNodeId>) and run the dispatcher core (SES mocked via aws-sdk-client-mock, suppression→cap→quiet-hours→verified-domain gate) → assert a messages_log row with campaign_id = the campaign, ses_message_id set, status='sent', and SES SendEmail called with the workspace Configuration Set AFTER the gates (never a real send).

WEBHOOK fired via the INJECTED client (never a real host): assert exactly one captured request to the allowlisted URL, method/headers/rendered body correct ({{customer.*}} merged), the encrypted auth header decrypted at call time only; an SSRF/blocked target (loopback/169.254/non-allowlist) is refused and the failure is ISOLATED (recorded in activity_log source='webhook', tick does not crash, enrollment still advances).

IDEMPOTENCY: re-running the final tick (same enrollmentId) does NOT create a second outbox row (UNIQUE dedupe_key), does NOT re-send (messages_log stays 1), does NOT re-fire the webhook (per-node dedupe marker), and the enrollment stays 'completed'.

WORKSPACE-SCOPING: a parallel WS_B campaign/enrollment with identical structure is untouched by the WS_A ticks; every write asserted scoped to the enrollment's workspace_id (runStatementsInWorkspaceTx guard); the dispatcher messages_log is in WS_A only.
testFiles.criterion: The full journey acceptance test passes: a built campaign enrolls (segment/event/manual), advances through wait/hour-window/if/send/webhook/exit with an injected clock, sends via the Dispatcher (messages_log, campaign_id) and fires the mocked webhook, idempotently, all workspace-scoped.

assertions: On #/campaigns/new build trigger→wait→hour_of_day_window→if→update-profile→send→webhook→exit via the (+) palette (reuse campaign-builder spec helpers), configure each node via its drawer editor (reuse campaign-node-editors patterns), attach a sendable template to the send node, configure the webhook node URL to a seeded-allowlisted host.
Save round-trips (toast); Publish flips status to 'active' on the list; the list row shows status 'active' + zero-initialized enrollment counts.
This is a thin browser smoke ONLY (no runner tick in-browser) — the advance/send/webhook assertions live in the integration test above.
testFiles.criterion: Full journey UI smoke: build the full-node journey in the browser and publish it (the runner advance is covered by the integration test).

assertions: Seed an active campaign + an enrollment due now; pause the campaign (status='paused'); runEnrollment(injected now) for that enrollment → result is a no-op/'skipped' (enrollment left at its node, status unchanged, no outbox/send) because the in-tx re-check requires the campaign status='active'.
Resume (status='active') then runEnrollment again → it advances normally (proves pause is a reversible halt, not a data mutation on the enrollment).

If the current runner does NOT yet read campaign.status inside the tick, this is the failing test that drives adding that gate in run.ts (load campaign status alongside definition in the locked read; honor 'active' only); idempotency/concurrency guarantees preserved.
testFiles.criterion: Lifecycle pause halts advancement: a paused campaign's due enrollment is not advanced by the runner.
